Here’s the /edit endpoint. Add this to index.js right before the /health route:  app.post("/edit", async (req, res) => {   const { file, old_text, new_text, description } = req.body;   if (!file || !old_text || new_text === undefined) {     return res.status(400).json({ error: "file, old_text, and new_text required" });   }    const filePath = path.join(__dirname, file);    try {     if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {       return res.status(404).json({ error: "File not found: " + file });     }      const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, "utf8");      if (!content.includes(old_text)) {       return res.status(400).json({ error: "old_text not found in file. No changes made." });     }      const occurrences = content.split(old_text).length - 1;     if (occurrences > 1) {       return res.status(400).json({ error: "old_text appears " + occurrences + " times. Be more specific." });     }      const updated = content.replace(old_text, new_text);     fs.writeFileSync(filePath, updated, "utf8");      log("file-edit", { file, description: description || "no description", old_text: old_text.slice(0, 100), new_text: new_text.slice(0, 100) });      res.json({ success: true, file, description: description || "edit applied" });    } catch (err) {     res.status(500).json({ error: "Edit failed: " + err.message });   } });  app.post("/edit-and-restart", async (req, res) => {   const { file, old_text, new_text, description } = req.body;   if (!file || !old_text || new_text === undefined) {     return res.status(400).json({ error: "file, old_text, and new_text required" });   }    const filePath = path.join(__dirname, file);    try {     if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {       return res.status(404).json({ error: "File not found: " + file });     }      const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, "utf8");      if (!content.includes(old_text)) {       return res.status(400).json({ error: "old_text not found in file. No changes made." });     }      const occurrences = content.split(old_text).length - 1;     if (occurrences > 1) {       return res.status(400).json({ error: "old_text appears " + occurrences + " times. Be more specific." });     }      const updated = content.replace(old_text, new_text);     fs.writeFileSync(filePath, updated, "utf8");      log("file-edit-restart", { file, description: description || "no description" });      res.json({ success: true, file, restarting: true, description: description || "edit applied" });      // Restart pm2 after response is sent     setTimeout(() => {       try {         execSync("pm2 restart all --update-env", { cwd: __dirname });       } catch (e) {         console.error("pm2 restart failed:", e.message);       }     }, 500);    } catch (err) {     res.status(500).json({ error: "Edit failed: " + err.message });   } });   Save, pm2 restart all --update-env. Two endpoints: 	•	/edit — makes the change, no restart 	•	/edit-and-restart — makes the change and restarts pm2 automatically Then wire it into Jarvis’s system prompt so it can call these endpoints itself when you ask it to make a code change. Add this to the COMPUTER ACTIONS section in buildSystemPrompt:  EDIT:{"file":"index.js","old_text":"exact text to replace","new_text":"replacement","description":"what this does"} EDIT_RESTART:{"file":"index.js","old_text":"exact text to replace","new_text":"replacement","description":"what this does"}   And add this to handleAction in index.js:    } else if (action.action === "edit") {     try {       const filePath = path.join(__dirname, action.file);       const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, "utf8");       if (!content.includes(action.old_text)) return "Edit failed: old_text not found";       const updated = content.replace(action.old_text, action.new_text);       fs.writeFileSync(filePath, updated, "utf8");       log("jarvis-self-edit", { file: action.file, description: action.description });       return "Edited " + action.file + ": " + (action.description || "done");     } catch (e) {       return "Edit failed: " + e.message;     }   } else if (action.action === "edit_restart") {     try {       const filePath = path.join(__dirname, action.file);       const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, "utf8");       if (!content.includes(action.old_text)) return "Edit failed: old_text not found";       const updated = content.replace(action.old_text, action.new_text);       fs.writeFileSync(filePath, updated, "utf8");       log("jarvis-self-edit-restart", { file: action.file, description: action.description });       setTimeout(() => execSync("pm2 restart all --update-env", { cwd: __dirname }), 500);       return "Edited " + action.file + " and restarting: " + (action.description || "done");     } catch (e) {       return "Edit failed: " + e.message;     }   }   Once this is in, you can just tell Jarvis “fix the Instacart pace alert to only fire after Wednesday” and it’ll make the surgical edit itself and restart. No more full file regeneration for small fixes.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

# Agent 09 — Architect  **Role:** Active housekeeper early, gated architect forever. Raises the floor on code quality across all of Jarvis by watching logs, finding inefficiencies, and either fixing small things itself or proposing bigger ones for Conor's approval.  **Core principle:** Logic the fix out *before* touching code. Every action — tiny or huge — has a reasoning log entry. No silent changes.  ---  ## Two speeds  ### Speed 1: Auto-fix (runs freely, no approval)  Small, safe, reversible changes. Agent-09 applies these itself and logs what it did. Conor sees a summary in the morning briefing.  **What qualifies:** - Dead code removal (unused imports, unreachable branches, commented-out blocks older than 30 days) - Log noise reduction (duplicate log lines, overly verbose debug logs) - Obvious typos in strings, comments, error messages - Consolidating duplicated helper functions into a shared util - Formatting / whitespace / consistent quote style - Scheduler jobs that log identical output every run (collapse to one entry) - Replacing hardcoded literals that appear 3+ times with a named constant - Fixing template-vs-rendered-string search traps (like the pace-alert grep loop) by adding inline comments near templated strings  **Hard limits on auto-fix:** - Max 5 files changed per run - Max 40 lines changed per run - Never touches: `agent-02-trader.js`, `agent-02b-autonomous.js`, `agent-04-health.js`, `autonomous-state.json`, `memory.json`, anything in `apex/` or med schedule logic - Never changes external API shapes (route signatures, response formats) - Never deletes files - Never modifies `package.json` or installs anything  Every auto-fix is a git commit with message `arch: <what> — <why>`. One commit per fix so reverts are clean.  ### Speed 2: Propose-and-wait (requires Conor's tap)  Anything structural, anything that touches trading/health/finance logic, anything over the auto-fix limits. Agent-09 writes a proposal, Conor approves in the UI, it applies.  **What qualifies:** - Refactors affecting 5+ files - New utility modules or shared abstractions - Route signature changes - Schema changes to `*.jsonl` logs or state files - Any change to agent-02, 02B, 04, or scheduler - Prompt changes for any agent using Opus - Anything the agent itself flags as "I'm not sure"  **Proposal format** (written to `proposals/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md`):  ``` # <title>  ## Problem What I noticed and where. Log evidence: <file:line refs>.  ## Why it matters Concrete impact: tokens wasted, time lost, bugs caused, confusion risked.  ## Proposed fix Prose description of the change.  ## Diff <actual git diff>  ## Risk What could break. What I'd watch for after applying.  ## Confidence Low / Medium / High + one sentence why. ```  Conor approves or rejects in the UI. Approval → apply + commit. Rejection → logged with reason, agent learns not to propose that class again.  ---  ## Early-phase acceleration  For the first 30 days, agent-09 runs **3x daily** instead of nightly:  - 6 AM — post pre-market scan, before trading hours - 1 PM — midday, while market is quiet-ish - 11 PM — full log sweep for the day  Rationale: floor is low right now. Lots of obvious wins. Frequent runs compound faster. After 30 days, evaluate — if auto-fix volume has dropped to a trickle, cut back to nightly.  ---  ## Inputs  - All `*.jsonl` log files (read-only) - Full repo tree (read; write only within auto-fix limits) - Last 7 days of scheduler output - Conor's approval/rejection history from past proposals (this is its training signal) - Any agent that emits a `self_concern` event (see "Inter-agent signals" below)  ## Outputs  - Auto-fix commits to git (tagged `arch:`) - Proposals to `proposals/` - Morning briefing injection: "Agent-09 overnight: N auto-fixes, M new proposals waiting" - `ARCHITECT_LOG.jsonl` — every run, every decision, every reasoning trace - `REJECTED_PATTERNS.jsonl` — things Conor said no to, with why  ---  ## Logic-before-action pattern (the non-negotiable)  Before any change (auto or proposed), agent-09 writes a reasoning block to `ARCHITECT_LOG.jsonl`:  ```json {   "ts": "2026-04-19T23:00:00Z",   "trigger": "log scan found 47 duplicate 'scheduler tick' entries from agent-03",   "hypothesis": "agent-03 is logging on every heartbeat, not on state change",   "evidence": ["logs/agent-03.jsonl:12041-12087", "agent-03-income.js:142"],   "options_considered": [     "remove log line entirely",     "change to log only on state change",     "collapse identical consecutive entries"   ],   "chosen": "log only on state change",   "reasoning": "keeps signal, removes noise, cheapest to implement",   "confidence": "high",   "action": "auto-fix",   "commit": "abc1234" } ```  If any field feels weak ("confidence: low" or "options_considered" has only one entry), the action auto-escalates to propose-and-wait regardless of size. Uncertainty → human gate.  ---  ## Inter-agent signals (cheap future hook)  Any agent can emit a `self_concern` event when it notices its own behavior is off — retrying the same failed call, looping, getting ambiguous results. Agent-09 reads these at next run and prioritizes investigating them.  Initial shape: ```json { "agent": "03", "concern": "RSS feed parse failing repeatedly", "ts": "...", "context": {...} } ```  No agent is required to emit these yet. Just the door, opened.  ---  ## Guardrails  - **Git reset is always one command away.** Every auto-fix is a single atomic commit. - **Budget cap:** agent-09 itself runs on Haiku. Opus only for proposal drafting (the writeup), not the log scanning. Hard cap: $2/day in API costs for agent-09. If exceeded, it goes dormant until the next UTC day. - **Kill switch on the UI chips strip.** Same row as the 02B kills. Tap to disable agent-09 until Conor re-enables. - **Read-only directories:** enforced at the tool layer, not just by prompt. If agent-09's edit tool is called with a path in the excluded list, the call fails. - **Daily proposal cap:** max 5 open proposals at once. Past 5, it stops writing new ones and prioritizes reminding Conor to review the backlog. - **Self-check:** every run starts by verifying its own config hasn't drifted (file hash check on agent-09's own source). If it has, it logs and refuses to run until Conor acknowledges.  ---  ## UI touchpoints (ties into the roadmap)  - **Chips strip:** add `ARCH: N` chip — number of open proposals. Tap opens proposal modal. Red if >3. - **Action row:** add button `Proposals` — same as tapping the chip. - **Proposal modal:** popup overlay, not a new page. Shows one proposal at a time with title, problem, diff (syntax highlighted), risk, confidence. Three buttons: Approve (applies + commits), Reject (logs reason), Defer (keeps in queue). If multiple proposals pending, stack navigation at top of modal. - **Briefing:** daily summary of auto-fixes applied + proposals awaiting review.  ---  ## Phased build  ### Phase A — Read-only observer (week 1) - Agent-09 scans logs 3x daily, writes `ARCHITECT_LOG.jsonl` only - No code changes. No proposals. Just watching. - Conor reads the log, sanity-checks the reasoning quality - Purpose: verify the agent sees what you'd want it to see before giving it hands  ### Phase B — Propose-only (week 2) - Agent-09 starts writing proposals to `proposals/` - Still no auto-fix. Every change goes through Conor. - Purpose: calibrate proposal quality before loosening the leash  ### Phase C — Auto-fix unlocked (week 3+) - Turn on auto-fix with full limits - Monitor ARCHITECT_LOG daily for the first week - Adjust limits up or down based on accuracy  ### Phase D — Steady state (month 2+) - Drop cadence to nightly if volume has dropped - Consider expanding auto-fix scope based on track record  ---  ## Success metric  **Floor metric:** number of "same annoying thing is still happening" complaints from Conor per week. Target: trend toward zero.  **Ceiling metric:** percentage of agent-09 proposals approved without edits. Target: 70%+ by month 2. Higher = agent understands the codebase well enough to be trusted with more autonomy.  ---  ## Resolved decisions  1. **Git:** unknown if initialized. Phase 0 step: check `git status` in repo root. If not a repo, `git init` + initial commit before agent-09 touches anything. No agent-09 auto-fix ever runs without a working git history behind it. 2. **Proposal surface:** `proposals/` lives at repo root (simplest, visible, easy to gitignore later if desired). Proposals surface in the UI as a **popup/modal** triggered by the `ARCH: N` chip or the Proposals button. Modal shows: title, problem, diff (syntax highlighted), risk, confidence → Approve / Reject / Defer buttons. One proposal at a time, stack behind if multiple. 3. **Budget:** $2/day Haiku ceiling confirmed. Revisit when autonomous trading starts generating income. 4. **Kat:** out of scope. Agent-09 is Conor-only for now. No UI surfaces Kat sees. Revisit when Jarvis is polished enough to demo.
